question: What was NVIDIA's total revenue in fiscal year 2023 (the year ended January 29, 2023), and how did it compare to the prior year?
expected_answer: $26,974 million, essentially flat versus $26,914 million in fiscal year 2022 (an increase of only about $60 million, or 0.2%)
notes: Verified. Tests retrieval of an earlier NVDA year (the flat year before the AI boom), distinct from v3's FY2024/FY2025 questions.

expected_answer: $13,507 million (approximately $13.5 billion)

question: How much cash and cash equivalents did Microsoft hold as of June 30, 2024?
expected_answer: $18,315 million (approximately $18.3 billion)

question: Why was NVIDIA's revenue roughly flat in fiscal year 2023 (ended January 2023), and what happened to its Gaming business that year?
expected_answer: Total revenue was essentially flat (~$26.97 billion vs ~$26.91 billion) because a sharp decline in Gaming revenue — driven by weaker demand, channel inventory corrections, and the collapse of cryptocurrency-related GPU demand — offset growth elsewhere (Data Center). The year was a consolidation period before the AI-driven surge of FY2024.
notes: Tests analytical retrieval from an earlier NVDA MD&A. Key theme: Gaming decline / crypto / inventory correction offsetting Data Center.

question: How did Apple return cash to shareholders in fiscal year 2024 through dividends and buybacks?
expected_answer: Apple paid $15,234 million in dividends and dividend equivalents and repurchased $94,949 million of common stock in fiscal year 2024 — roughly $110 billion returned to shareholders in total.
notes: Verified. Tests combining two cash-flow line items (dividends + buybacks) into one answer.

question: What does Microsoft's More Personal Computing segment include?
expected_answer: The More Personal Computing segment covers Windows (OEM licensing and commercial), Devices (including Surface and PC accessories), Gaming (including Xbox hardware and content/services, and Activision Blizzard), and Search and news advertising (Bing).
notes: Tests retrieval of segment composition from the business section. Acceptable if it captures Windows, Devices/Surface, Gaming/Xbox, and Search/Bing.

question: Which company repurchased more of its own stock in 2023: Alphabet or Meta?
expected_answer: Alphabet repurchased far more — $61,504 million versus Meta's $19,774 million (Alphabet bought back roughly 3x as much).
notes: Cross-company comparison from cash flow statements. Verified figures.

question: Compare Alphabet's and Meta's capital expenditures in 2023 — which was higher?
expected_answer: Alphabet's capital expenditures were higher at $32,251 million versus Meta's $27,266 million.
notes: Cross-company capex comparison. Verified figures.

question: Which company reported more cash and cash equivalents on its most recent fiscal year-end balance sheet: Apple (FY2024) or Microsoft (FY2024)?
expected_answer: Apple reported more cash and cash equivalents at $29,943 million versus Microsoft's $18,315 million. (Note: this is only the cash-and-equivalents line item; both companies hold substantial additional marketable securities.)
notes: Cross-company balance-sheet comparison with explicit period disambiguation. Verified figures.
